text:
Many great and wise teachings have been handed down to us in the Scriptures, that is, in the Law, the Prophets, and the other writings.[1] We should praise earlier generations of Israel for writing and saving these books. And if you really love to study the Scriptures, it is very important for you to be able to explain them to others, whether by talking or by writing. That's why my grandfather Jesus studied the Scriptures until he became an expert, then wrote this book to help others learn about wisdom. So if you love to learn, you should study this book, and you will come closer and closer to living as the Law requires. But I ask you to read with a good attitude and not to be critical of my translation. I worked hard at it, so be understanding in those places where it isn't perfect. The meaning of any Hebrew book changes a little when it is translated, whether it is from the Law or the Prophets or is one of the other books in the Scriptures. I came to Egypt in the thirty-eighth year of King Euergetes, and I stayed there for as long as he was king.[2] While I was there, I studied copies of many books, and that's when I realized I should translate this one. Translating carefully takes a lot of hard work, and I spent many sleepless nights before my translation was completed. And now that it is finished, those who live outside of Judea can read this book to learn how best to obey God's Law in their own lives. ⓐ Only the Lord can give Wisdom,
and his Wisdom lasts forever.

And who but the Lord
can count the raindrops
or the days in eternity
or the sand on the seashore?

And who else can measure
the height of heaven
or the width of the earth
or the depth of the sea?

The Lord created Wisdom
long before anything else.

The Wisdom in God's commands
flows from his word in heaven
like streams from a spring.[3]

Who can understand
the deep thoughts of Wisdom
or how clever she[4] is?

Can anyone be taught
all of her knowledge?
Can anyone understand
how much she has learned?[5]

Only the Lord is that wise;
he is fearsome and majestic
as he rules from his throne.

ⓑ The Lord created Wisdom
and watched her grow,
then he gave her
to his creation—

to every living thing—
but especially to those people
who love him.[6]

If you fear the Lord,
you will be proud
of the honors you receive,
and you will wear happiness
like a crown.

And if you fear him,
your life will be long
and filled with joy.[7]

You will die happy,
blessed by the Lord
on the day of your death.

Respect and obey the Lord—
that's where Wisdom begins,
and she will stay
with faithful people
from the day of their birth.

She has lived among us humans
since the beginning of time,
and she will never desert
our descendants.

Respect and obey the Lord—
this is complete Wisdom.
Be drunk on the wine
of Wisdom,

ⓒ and let her fill your house
and your barns
with the harvest she brings.

Wisdom will give you
respect for the Lord,
to be worn like a crown
woven from the flowers
of peace and good health.[8]

Hold on to Wisdom,
and she will honor you
by sending knowledge
and complete understanding
like showers of rain.

ⓓ Wisdom is like a tree—
at its root is respect
for the Lord,
and its branches represent
a long lifetime.

Fear of the Lord
forces sin and anger
out of your life.[9]

It is never right to be angry
without a good reason,
because anger will start you
down the path to disaster.

Just be patient and stay calm
until your anger fades.

Then when the time is right
you can speak,
and others will tell
of your good sense.

Wisdom collects wise sayings,
but faithfulness to God
is disgusting to a sinner.

If you want lots of Wisdom
from the Lord,
then obey his commands.

Respect and obey the Lord,
and you will learn Wisdom;
be faithful and humble,
and he will be pleased.

Faithfully obey the Lord
and worship him
with all your heart.

Others are watching you,
so be careful—
don't say one thing
and then do something else.

If you boast and then fail,
you will be disgraced.
And if you only pretend
to worship the Lord,
he will bring you down
and show everyone
that you are a fake.

Students, if you decide
to worship and obey the Lord,
be ready to face problems.

Have firm self-control
and don't act too quickly
if trouble strikes.

Don't turn from the Lord,
but be faithful;
that's the way to succeed.

Accept everything that happens.
Be patient if others
humiliate you—

ⓔ humiliation tests people
as a furnace tests gold.[10]

The Lord will give his help,
if you depend only on him
and do right.

If you fear the Lord
and are patient,
he will show you mercy.
But if you turn away,
you will fall.

Put your trust in him,
and he will reward you.

Don't give up hoping
for good things, like mercy
and a life full of joy.

Look at past generations—
did the Lord desert those people
who trusted and feared him?
Or did he fail to answer those
who asked him for help?

The Lord is kind
and shows mercy
by forgiving our sins
and rescuing us from trouble.

But the Lord will send trouble
if you don't have the courage
to be faithful
and to stay away from sin.

If you are too afraid
to put your trust in him,
he will not protect you
when problems come.

And if you give up,
you will be in for trouble
when the Lord decides
to judge your life. *

ⓕ If you love the Lord
and respect him,
you will obey his commands.

You will try to please him
and to be completely faithful
to his Law.

If you fear the Lord,
you will prepare yourself
to worship him
with humility.

We pray, Lord, that you
will decide our future.
You are as merciful
as you are majestic,[11]
so don't leave our fate
in human hands.

Students, I have been
like a father to you,
and you will be safe
if you do what I say.

The Lord has told children
to honor and obey
their parents.

If you honor your father,
your sins will be forgiven;

show respect to your mother,
and you will be truly rich.

Honor your father—
then your own children
will make you happy,
and God will hear them
when they pray.

If you want to live a long time,
then you must obey the Lord
by respecting your parents;

serve them as if you
were their slave.

ⓖ If you speak to your father
with respect
and do what he asks,
he will bless you,

and your own family
will have success.
But if your mother
places a curse on you,
your family will face ruin.

You cannot get respect
by disgracing your parents.

What brings honor to them
also brings honor to you.

Help your father
as he grows old;
try not to upset him.

And when you are strong,
but his mind is fading,
don't make fun—
instead, be patient.

God sees your kindness,
and right away,
some of your sins
will be forgiven.

And if you later have troubles
because of your sins,
they will all melt away
in the warmth of your kindness,
like frost in the sunshine.

But abandoning your father
or making your mother angry
is as bad as cursing the Lord,
and so he will curse you.

Students, be humble
in your work,
and you will be loved
by God's people.

ⓗ The more powerful you are,
the more humble you must be
if you want the Lord
to be kind to you.

Many people are important
and famous,
but God gives his secrets
only to the humble.[12]

God is extremely powerful,
and he will accept praise only
from those who are not proud.

Don't try to learn things
you cannot understand.

ⓘ Instead, think about God's Law
and don't worry about secrets
God keeps to himself.

He has already shown you
matters beyond understanding,
and anything more
is none of your business.

Proud thoughts have led many
to turn from following God,
and evil ideas have ruined
the way they think.

Without eyes, you cannot see,
and without gaining knowledge
you can never get wisdom.

If you are stubborn,
you will end up in trouble,
and if you love danger,
it will kill you someday.

Those who are stubborn
are burdened with suffering,
and sinners carry
a heavy load of sins.

Evil has taken root
inside the arrogant,
and so when disaster strikes,
they cannot be rescued.

The wise always try to listen,
and they will pay attention
to a wise saying.

Money given to the poor
brings forgiveness for sins,
just as water
puts out a fire.

If you think that in the future
you might ever need help,
then repay other people now
when they do favors for you.

Students, if the poor
work for you,
don't cheat on their wages,
but pay them right away.

Don't disappoint the hungry
or upset the needy.

And if they are upset already,
don't make things worse
by being slow in giving them
what they need.

When people ask for help,
don't turn them down;
and if others are in need,
don't turn away.

If you pretend not to notice,
they will have good reason
to curse you.

And if their curses flow
from hearts you made bitter,
God, the Creator, will hear them
and do what they ask.

Be friendly to everyone
in the community
and show proper respect
to the town leaders.

When the poor greet you,
be polite and greet them
in return.

Rescue everyone
who suffers from injustice;
have the courage
to make the right decisions;

provide for orphans and widows,
as if they were your own
children or wife.
Then you will be a true child
of God Most High,
and he will love you more
than your own mother does.

If you study Wisdom,
you will become her child,
and she will help you
be successful.

If you love life
and want to find happiness,
love Wisdom enough
to get up early and study.

Hold tightly to her—
you will receive glory,
and the Lord will bless
everyone around you.

To obey her is to worship
the Holy Lord God,
and if you love Wisdom,
the Lord will love you.

Pay attention and obey Wisdom;
you will live in safety
and even rule nations.

Remain faithful to her,
and she will belong to you
and your descendants.

At first, Wisdom will test you
with her commands,
and you will walk in fear
along twisting pathways.
Her discipline
will bring suffering
until you are truly faithful.

But then she will return
straight to you,
bringing happiness
and telling her secrets.

But if you wander away,
she will let you go off
to your doom.

Be on your guard against evil,
but use every opportunity
for doing good.
And don't be ashamed
of who you are.

To be ashamed of your sins
leads to forgiveness
and honor,
but some other kinds of shame
are sins themselves.

If you play favorites
or let bullies push you around,
you will get hurt.

If you can make things right
by speaking up, then do so.
Don't let the opinions of others
make you hide your wisdom.[13]

If your wisdom and knowledge
are to make any difference,
you will have to speak.

Don't ever try
to make the truth seem false,
but always be humble
about things you don't know.

Don't try to hide your sins
from God—
it would be easier
to stop a river from flowing.

Never promise to obey a fool,
and don't play favorites
while a ruler is watching.

And even if you risk your life
fighting for truth,
the Lord God will be there,
fighting by your side.

Don't be arrogant
in what you say,
or slow and weak
in what you do.

When you are at home,
don't be fierce as a lion
or suspicious of servants.

ⓙ Don't hold out your hand
when it's time to receive,
then close your fist
when it's time to give.

Don't trust your wealth
and say,
“I don't need anyone's help.

I'm strong and smart enough
to get anything I want,

and no one has the power
to make me obey.”
Talk like that,
and God will punish you!

ⓚ And don't reply, “I sinned,
but God didn't punish me.”
God was being patient.

Never be so sure
of his forgiveness
that you keep on sinning

and say, “God is very kind,
and he will forgive me
even though I sin a lot.”
Yes, God is kind,
but he also gets angry
and punishes sinners.

Turn back to the Lord now—
don't wait until tomorrow.
If he gets angry,
he will quickly punish you
with death.

And wealth gained by cheating
won't save you from disaster.

Not every wind is good
for blowing away husks
from threshed grain.
And not every path leads
where you want to go.
But sinners will say anything
to get what they want.[14]

Stand up for what you believe,
and don't change what you say
from one time to the next.

Be willing to listen to anyone,
but think before you answer.

Then speak up, if you know
what to say;
and if not, just keep quiet.

Your words can bring you honor,
but they can also cause
your ruin and disgrace.

When you speak,
don't tear others down
or try to make them say
something wrong.
You would receive
severe punishment,
far worse than the shame
felt by thieves.

So don't make big mistakes,
or even little ones,

that turn you into an enemy
instead of a friend.
A reputation for being dishonest
will bring you nothing
but shame and insults.

Don't fall under the power
of evil desires.
They are burning flames
that will destroy you,[15]

and you will be left to wither,
like a tree with burned leaves
and scorched fruit.

Evil desires will destroy you,
and your enemies will laugh.

If you speak pleasantly
and politely,
you will make friends
and be welcome anywhere.

Be friendly with everyone,
but only listen to advice
from those few you can trust.

Make sure they are true friends
before you trust them,

because some will be friendly
when everything is fine,
but they won't help
when you have trouble.

If you argue with other friends,
they will become enemies
and tell about the argument
so you will look bad.

Still others will come to dinner
but not to help
if you have trouble.

In good times,
they will be so close,
that your servants
will have to obey them.

But if you lose your wealth,
they will disappear.

Keep your distance
from enemies
and don't be fooled
by false friends.
*

Loyal friends will protect you
with all their strength

and are worth more
than any other treasure.
*

They are even like medicine
that can save your life.
And here's how you can have
such loyal friends:

obey the Lord and choose friends
who also obey him.

Students, be disciplined
and learn Wisdom
when you are young,
and as your hair turns gray,
she will never desert you.

ⓛ Learning Wisdom isn't so hard.
It's like plowing a little
and planting a few seeds—
then a short time later
eating food from the harvest.

But to those who are fools
or have no discipline,
the job will seem too great,
and they will give up.

When Wisdom tests them,
they will toss her aside,
as if she were a stone
too heavy to carry.

Wisdom is hidden
from most people,
just as her name suggests.[16]

Pay attention, students—
don't reject my advice.

Become a slave to Wisdom,
with her chains fastened
around your neck and feet.

Carry her on your shoulders
and don't let those chains
make you angry.

Make up your mind to follow
and stay close to Wisdom
wherever she goes.

Search, and you will find her;
once she is yours,
hold her tightly.

Then she will give you
a peaceful and happy life.

Her chains on your feet
will become your protection,
and those on your neck
will turn into a royal robe,

decorated with gold jewelry
and tied with a purple cord.

You will wear Wisdom
like a beautiful robe
or like a crown
at a victory celebration.

Students, you are able
to discipline yourself
and learn Wisdom—
just put your mind to it!

Do you love to listen?
That's all it really takes
to gain Wisdom.

Spend time with older people
and stay close to those
who are wise.

When godly people speak,
pay attention, and memorize
every wise saying you hear.

If you know smart people,
visit them whenever you can,
until their welcome mat
wears thin from your feet.

ⓜ Never stop considering
God's laws and commands,
and he will strengthen your mind
with the Wisdom you want.

If you don't do evil,
then evil cannot harm you.

Always be fair with others,
and injustice will stay
away from you.

But those who farm
the fields of injustice,
will harvest seven times more
than they plant.

Don't ask the Lord
to make you a leader,
and don't ask the king
to give you honor.

Don't try to impress the Lord
with your goodness,
or to show off your wisdom
in front of the king.

Don't seek to be a judge
unless you have the strength
to do away with injustice.
And if you don't have courage
to stand up to the powerful,
you will ruin your good name.

Don't disgrace yourself
by sinning
against your community.

You won't escape punishment
if you commit a sin even once,
much less twice.

And do not think
that God Most High
will pardon you,
just because you have offered
a lot of sacrifices.

Don't let anything
discourage you from praying
or giving to the poor.

God can take away your success
as easily as he gave it,
so do not make fun of anyone
who is bitter from suffering.

Do not tell lies to a relative
or a friend

or anyone else.
No good ever comes
from telling lies.

In a town council meeting,
don't talk too much.
And in your prayers,
don't repeat the same things
over and over again.

Enjoy all kinds of hard work,
especially farming, because
God Most High created it.

Do not join others in sinning—
just think of how quickly
God can punish.

So be as humble as you can,
because those who are ungodly
will be eaten by worms
and burned by fire.

Don't betray a friend for money
or a relative for pure gold.[17]

If your wife is wise and good
and gracious,
she is more valuable than gold,
so do not divorce her.

If your workers or slaves
work hard and well,
don't mistreat them.

ⓝ If you have wise slaves,
love them as much
as you love yourself
and set them free
when you are supposed to.[18]

If you have cattle,
give them good care
and keep them as long
as they bring you a profit.

While your children are young,
teach them to obey you.

Do not let a daughter
always have her own way,
but protect her from men.

Finding a husband for her
is important, but be certain
to find one who is wise.

Don't divorce your wife
if she pleases you,
but if you can't stand her,
then don't trust her.

ⓞ Honor your father
with all your heart
and remember that your birth
gave your mother great pain.

How can you ever repay them
for the gift of life?

Fear the Lord
with all that you are,
and respect his priests.

Put all your strength
into loving your Creator
and be sure that his servants
make a good living.

Fear the Lord
and honor his priests
with what he has commanded.
Bring them the first part
of your harvest
and the sacrifice
to make things right.
Give them the shoulders
from other sacrifices
and all offerings and holy food
dedicated to the Lord.

The Lord will give you
complete success,
if you give to the poor.

So be generous to everyone,
especially to those
whose loved ones have died.[19]

Go to see them
and weep as they mourn.

If you visit the sick,
everyone will love you.

You can keep from sinning
by always reminding yourself
that you will someday die.

Don't fight those in power
if you have no chance to win.

Don't argue with those
who have plenty of gold—
they will drag you down
by bribing others
or by persuading the king
to see things their way.

And arguing with those
who talk too much
is like piling more wood
on a fire.

Don't laugh at those
who are crude,
unless you want to hear them
insult your ancestors.

If people are trying
to stop committing sins,
don't make them feel guilty;
remember that we all deserve
to be punished for our sins.

Don't dishonor old people—
the rest of us
are getting older too.

Never be happy
when someone dies.
Remember, all of us
must die someday.

When wise people speak,
pay attention, then study
what they have said.
You will learn discipline
and how to serve
those in power.

Listen when old people tell you
what they learned
from their ancestors.
Then you will understand
how to give the best answer
when one is needed.

Don't encourage sinners to sin;
they might blaze up like a fire
and you would get burned.

Don't argue with the arrogant—
they will try to turn
your own words against you.

Never lend to someone
who is stronger than you;
if you make such a loan,
don't count on being repaid.

Don't ever guarantee a loan
for more than you can afford,
and don't be surprised
if you have to repay
a loan you guaranteed.

Never sue judges—
their influence is so great
that they will always win.

Don't travel with those
who like danger.
They will do what they please
and cause you problems,
and their foolishness
might get you killed.

Don't start a fight
with hot-tempered people.
They don't care who they kill,
so don't travel with them
along deserted roads,
where you would be helpless
if they attacked.

Fools can't keep a secret,
so don't ask them for advice.

And when strangers are around
don't do anything
that you want kept secret—
you never know
who they might tell.

Do not discuss with just anyone
what you really think,
and don't let others
do favors for you.

ⓟ Love your wife,
but don't act jealous—
that will only invite her
to hurt you.

If you let yourself fall
under the spell of a woman,
she will walk all over you.

You had best stay away
from an immoral woman,
or you will be caught
in her traps.

Do not spend too much time
with a woman who sings
to entertain men—
she can use many tricks
to take control of you.

ⓠ Do not gaze too long
at a virgin—
if you talk her into having sex,
you will have to pay a fine
and marry her.

Don't let yourself be controlled
by a desire for prostitutes,
or you will lose everything
that you inherited.

So don't look for them;
stay off the lonely streets
where they are found.

Don't stare at a married woman
with a beautiful figure.
A woman's beauty
can set men on fire
and has led many astray.

Never have dinner or drinks
with someone else's wife.
To fall in love with her
is to fall to your death.

Don't desert old friends—
like bottles of aged wine,
they bring cheer
more than new ones.

Don't be jealous of sinners
who receive praise;
you never can tell
how their life will end.

Don't look for happiness
where ungodly people do.
You know they won't always
get away with their sins.

Stay away from those
who are able to kill you,
and you won't live in fear
of death.
To be near them is to walk
through a field full of traps
or across a ledge
high on the city walls—
make one false step
and your life is over.

Do your best to know
your close neighbors,
but only ask the wise ones
for advice.

Speak with the wise
about the Law
of God Most High.

Ask godly people over for dinner
and be proud
that you fear the Lord.

Craftsmen receive praise
if their work shows skill,
and the leaders of a nation
will be honored
for wise words.

But loud, reckless leaders
are hated and feared.

A wise judge teaches the laws
to the people,
and a smart ruler
gets things organized.

The people of a town
and its lower officials
usually follow the example
set by the judges and rulers.

A stupid king
destroys his nation,
but a city grows
if its leaders can rule well.

The Lord controls
all authorities on earth,
and he appoints the best rulers
for each situation and time.

God alone gives the success
that brings honor to those
who write the laws.

When neighbors harm you,
don't be angry or proud.

The Lord and all people
hate pride and become angry
with injustice.

A wealthy and arrogant nation
that fails to show justice
will soon be conquered.

Why are we humans so proud?
We are only dust and ashes,
and we easily lose
our health and strength.[20]

A long illness mocks the skill
of physicians,[21]
and even a king
can die without warning.

Then after our death,
our bodies are eaten
by wild animals and maggots
and worms.

We humans start to be arrogant
when our hearts turn away
from the Lord our Creator.

Sins are the source of pride,
and if we hold on to it,
our lives will be flooded
with more disgusting sins.
So the Lord destroys[22] the proud
by punishing them with disasters
they never dreamed possible.

ⓡ He removes powerful rulers,
replacing them with
the humble.

When God decides to give land
to a humble nation,
he pulls up others by the roots

and plows through their fields
until nothing remains.

The Lord destroys some of them
so completely, that no one
remembers they ever lived.

When the Lord created humans,
he did not intend for us
to be proud and angry.

We deserve honor
only when we fear the Lord,
but we should not be honored
if we break his laws.

A family should give honor
to its leader,
but God honors all
who fear him.

He accepts them,
but he rejects those
who are proud and stubborn.[23]

Fearing the Lord is something
to be proud of—
whether you are poor,
or rich and famous,
a Jew, or a Gentile.

Don't look down on those
who are wise but poor,
and don't honor sinners.

Honor always comes to judges
and people in power,
but anyone who fears the Lord
is even more deserving.

So if you are smart,
you will understand
when you see a master
serving a wise servant.

Don't spend your time thinking
of ways to avoid work,
and don't pretend to be well off
if you are short of money.

It's better to work
and have more than enough
than to boast and go hungry.

Students, be humble
but remember to have
proper self-esteem.

Who will think you are innocent
if you call yourself guilty?
And who will give you honor
if you refuse
to respect yourself?

The poor receive honor
if they are educated,
while the wealthy are praised
because they have money.

So when the poor become rich
they will be praised more,
but the disgrace of the wealthy
will become much worse
if they lose their money.

Those in power
always make room
for someone wise and humble.

Do not praise or despise anyone
on the basis of their looks.

The honeybee is very small,
but it makes
the sweetest food.

Don't boast about your clothes
or act important
when you are honored.
Only the Lord does things
truly worthy of praise,
but he keeps them hidden
from our sight.

Kings often lose their power
to those who had seemed
to be nobodies,

while honored rulers
are captured and disgraced.

Find out the truth
before you criticize.

Don't interrupt others—
hear them out,
then give your answer.

If it's none of your business,
then don't argue with them,
and stay away from sinners
when they discuss what to do.

Students, don't let yourself
become too busy.
If you do, you will sin
and not find success
or escape trouble.

Some people work all day long
as hard as they can,
but they keep getting poorer.

Others are poor and weak;
they work slowly
and always need help.
But the Lord shows kindness

and to everyone's surprise,
he lifts them from poverty
and gives them honor.

All things, whether good or bad,
come from the Lord.
He gives life and riches
or takes them away.

The Lord gives wisdom
by teaching us
to understand his Law.
He helps us love each other
and do right.

Sinners are dishonest
and live in darkness,
proud of their evil
from the day they are born,
and so evil will stay with them
until the day they die.[24]

But the Lord gives success
to his faithful people
and blesses them with gifts
that last forever.

ⓢ You can become rich,
if you work hard
and never spend any money.
But then what?

When you finally retire
to enjoy what you own,
you soon may die,
and then your wealth
will belong to others.

When you agree to do work,
stay busy and don't quit.

Don't be surprised
at what sinners do;
trust the Lord and keep working,
because he can make you rich
in a moment's time.

Suddenly, he will reward you
and bless you with prosperity,
if you are faithful to him.
*

So don't say, “I already have
everything I need—
what more could the Lord
do for me?
Nothing can harm me now!”

When people are well off,
they never mention
how needy they once were,
and when they have trouble,
the good times are forgotten.

But at the end of life,
the Lord will pay us back
for what we have done.

Pleasant memories fade
after an hour of pain,
but the way that you lived
is made clear by how you die

and by what kind of children
you leave behind.
So don't praise anyone
until they are dead.

Don't let just anyone
come to your house.
Dishonest people will trick you,

as bird hunters use decoys
to trap quails.
They watch you like spies,
waiting for some mistake

or for a chance
to make your good deeds
seem evil.

One spark can start a fire,
and one sinner's evil plan
can end in murder.

Watch out for the schemes
of those who do evil,
or they may ruin
your good name.

And if you let foreigners
come to live in your home,
they will cause trouble
and persuade you to accept
their customs and gods.[25]

ⓣ Do good to those
who deserve it,
and they will thank you.

Be kind to the godly,
and they—or God Most High—
will reward you.

But you will have no success
if you keep doing evil
and refuse to give
to the poor.
*

Help those who are humble
and faithful to God,

but don't help the ungodly.
If you give food to sinners,
they will become strong
and overpower you.
Do a good deed for them,
and they will reward you
by wronging you twice.

But God Most High
hates ungodly sinners
and will punish them.

Yes, give to good people,
not to sinners.

In hard times you will discover
your true friends
and your real enemies.

Even your enemies act friendly
when you are well off,
but only true friends stay
when trouble strikes.

Never trust your enemies—
their evil will attack you
like rust

eating away
at an unpolished mirror.[26]
So be on guard against them,
even if they bow down
and act humble.

Don't make them your partners,
or they will push you aside
and take over.
Then you will know I was right,
and you will feel the sting
of my words.

If you try to charm snakes
or to tame wild animals,
who will take pity
when you get bitten?

In the same way,
no one will take pity
if you are friendly with sinners
and start sinning yourself.

Enemies act loyal for a while,
but they won't stand by you
when you need help.

They tell you how much
they want to be your friends,
all the while plotting
to put you in your grave.
They weep when you are sad,
but they will try to kill you
the first chance they get.

If you are struck by disaster,
they pretend to help
but pull you down instead.

Then they laugh and sneer,
making vulgar hand signs,
showing what kind of friends
they really are.

Can you stay humble
if your friends are proud?
Only if you can handle tar
and stay clean!

And watch out if your friends
are rich and powerful.
You cannot match their strength,
and you might be crushed,
just like a clay jar
struck by an iron pan.

The rich oppress
and insult the poor,
then demand an apology!

The rich will try to use you,
but if you need help,
don't bother to ask.

When they come to visit you,
they will soak up
what little you have.

If they need your help,
they smile and act kind
and even ask how they
can be of service.
But don't be fooled—
it's all lies!

They will invite you to dinner,
but in the long run you'll pay
three times what it cost.
And when you have nothing left,
they will laugh and sneer
and keep their distance.

So be smart and careful,
not deceived and humiliated.

Important people
will want you around,
if you don't try too hard
to be their friend.

Push yourself on them,
and you will face rejection;
act cold, and they will forget
that you are alive.

Don't try to be their equal
or trust them when they
have long talks with you.
They are smiling,
but you are being tested.

Those who tell secrets are cruel
and would just as soon hurt you
or send you to prison.

Be careful! Don't risk disaster
by sharing your secrets.

Wake up and pay attention!
Every day of your life,
love the Lord and pray
that he will protect you.[27] *
Animals and people
love their own kind.

Wolves and lambs
have about as much in common
as sinners and God's people.

Hyenas and dogs
are natural enemies,
just like the poor
and the rich.

Desert lions will always feast
on wild donkeys,
and the rich will gobble up
the needy.

The rich and proud
consider the poor and humble
to be disgusting.

When the rich start to stumble
their friends lend a hand,
but let the poor fall down,
and their friends pretend
not to know them.

When the rich are in trouble,
many will offer to help
and will even make excuses
when the rich insult them.
But let the poor get in trouble,
and all they get is criticism,
even if they speak wisely.

When the rich speak,
everyone listens
with great respect.
But if the poor speak,
everyone asks, “Just who
do they think they are?”
And if the poor start to trip,
people give them a push.

It's good to have wealth
if you got it honestly,
but don't be ungodly and claim
that being poor is evil.

With one look at your face
anyone can see
if you are happy or sad.

A smile shows you are happy,
and a tired look may mean
you have been trying too hard
to think up a proverb.

You will be happiest
if you keep from sinning
and saying stupid things,

and if you have no reason
to feel guilty
and have not given up hope. *

What's the use of being rich
if you don't enjoy
spending money on yourself?

Are you just saving it
so others can live in luxury
after you are dead and gone?

Those who refuse to enjoy
a few things money can buy,
probably won't give much
to someone in need.

They spend nothing,
and punish themselves
with the greatest misery. *

If they do a good deed,
it is only by accident,
and the end of their life
shows how evil they were:

they pretended not to notice
others in need;

they were never happy
with their own fair share;
and by taking
what belonged to others,
their souls shriveled away.

They wouldn't even give
their families enough to eat!

Be good to yourself
according to what you have
and be generous in your giving
to the Lord.

You cannot know the time
for you to die,
and when it arrives,
death will not wait,

so now is the time
to help your friends
with generous gifts.

Every day, you should enjoy
the good things you want,

because after you are gone,
others will divide up everything
you worked so hard to get.

Give and accept gifts,
and treat yourself well,
because there is nothing good
in the world of the dead.

Every living creature wears out
like old clothes
and obeys the ancient law
that says, “You will die.”

Generations pass away,
and others are born
to take their place,
just as new leaves
on a tall tree
replace those that fall.

Everything you ever made
will someday turn to dust,
then even the memory of you
will disappear.

ⓤ Wisdom always blesses those
who learn how to reason
by thinking about her.

They consider
what Wisdom has done
and the secrets she knows.

They wait for her
like hunters along a path,
then they follow her

home,
where they peek
through the windows
and listen at the door.
*

They set up camp
so close to her house,
that their tent pegs
are hammered into its walls.

There is no better place to live
than in her home—

like a shade tree, she protects
the children of her followers

from the heat,
and they live in a place
of great beauty.

If you fear the Lord
and obey his Law,
you will find Wisdom
when you search.

She will care for you
like your own mother
and welcome you, as a bride
welcomes her husband.

And instead of something
to eat and drink,
she will give you wisdom
and understanding.

You can depend on her
to keep you from disgrace.

She will tell you what to say,
and you will be honored
because of your wise speeches
in town meetings.

Your life will be happy,
and you will be remembered
for all time.

But fools and sinners
will never find Wisdom.

She won't come near the proud
or enter the mind of a liar.

That's why the Lord
refuses to accept praise
from sinners.

Only the wise can offer
acceptable praise.

ⓥ The Lord hates it when you say
that he forced you to sin.

And he doesn't like sinners,
so don't claim he tempted you
to become one.

The Lord and all who fear him
hate disgusting sins.

The Lord created us humans,
and from the very beginning,
he gave us the ability
to choose how we will live.

You are able to obey his Law
if you really want to,
and you can please him
by being faithful.
*

Life and death are as different
as fire and water,

and the Lord will give you
whichever you choose.

The Lord is extremely wise
and tremendously powerful.
He can see all things—

every deed that we do—
and if we fear him,
he will watch over us.

He has neither commanded
nor given permission for anyone
to be evil or to sin.

Ungodly children are worthless
and are nothing to desire
or to celebrate.

You can have lots of children,
but they will bring joy
only if they fear the Lord.

Having lots of ungodly children
won't help you in old age—
you will outlive them all.
One good child,
or even no children at all,
is better than a thousand
who are ungodly.

A family that disobeys the law
can make a city a ghost town,
but one smart person can fill
an empty city with people.

Here are but a few
of the important things
I have seen and heard:

If a small group of sinners
sets the Lord's anger ablaze,
then it will be worse by far
if a whole nation is sinful.

ⓦ Long ago, the Lord refused
to forgive the mighty giants
who rebelled against him.

And he so hated the arrogance
of the people of Sodom,[28]
that he destroyed them.

They were doomed
because of their sins,
and the Lord showed no mercy
as he swept them away.

Nor did he have pity
on six hundred thousand
stubborn Assyrian soldiers
who attacked his people.

Even if only one person
refused to obey the Lord,
I would be surprised if he
did not punish that one.
The Lord loves to show mercy
and to use his mighty power
to forgive his people,
but when his anger overflows
against sinners,

he uses that same power
to punish them.
Everyone will receive
either mercy or punishment
as each deserves.

The Lord refuses to let sinners
profit from their crimes,
and he will reward his people
if they patiently wait.

He remembers every good deed
and gives everyone
what they deserve.

The Lord wanted everyone
to know his mighty power,
so he put a stubborn heart
in the king of Egypt.

But the Lord also wants everyone
to know about his mercy,
so he separated those
who have his light
from those who remain
in darkness.[29]

Never say to yourself,
“The Lord cannot see me.
He doesn't even know my name.
There are just too many people
for him to keep me in mind.

One glance from him
causes quaking and shaking
in the earth and the sky,
in the highest heaven
and the deepest sea,

in the mountains
and the foundations
of the earth.

But no human can understand
how his mighty power works.

Most of what God does
cannot be seen,
just like the wind
in a violent storm.

So who will tell him
when I do what is right?
And who has the patience
to wait for him
to keep his promises?”

That is how fools speak—
they cannot think straight
and have no understanding.

Listen, students;
pay attention,
and you will learn.

I will train you and tell you
exactly what I know.

In the beginning, when the Lord
created the world,
he gave each thing
its proper place.

He designed the forces of nature
to last for all time—
they never tire or get hungry
or stop working.

They don't get in the way
of each other,
and they will never
disobey him.

After God created the earth,
he looked it over
and filled it with creatures
that were good;

animals of all kinds
covered the ground,
and when they die,
they return to it.

ⓧ The Lord created us humans
from the soil,
and he makes us return there
when we die.

Our lives are short,
but the Lord has given us
power to rule the earth.

We were made to be like him
and were given power
much like his own.

He let us rule all animals
and made them afraid of us.

We were given the five senses
of sight and hearing,
taste, touch, and smell;
and we were also given
the ability to think
and to interpret
what our senses tell us.[30]

He gave us tongues for speaking,
eyes for seeing,
and minds for thinking
and for making choices.

ⓨ Our minds are filled
with knowledge and Wisdom
that come from the Lord,
and we understand the difference
between right and wrong.

The Lord made us fear him
so that we would know
his mighty power.

Now we will tell others
about his powerful deeds

and praise his holy name.[31]
*

The Lord gave to our ancestors
laws that bring knowledge
and life,

and his agreement with them
will last forever.

Our ancestors saw
the glorious power of the Lord
and heard his mighty voice

warning them about injustice
and commanding them how
to treat each other.

The Lord watches us and knows
our every deed.

Most people do wrong
from an early age
and have a cruel heart
that they cannot change.[32]

The Lord placed rulers
over all the nations,
but he himself rules Israel.

He raised our people Israel
as his own first-born child,
with loving care,
but also with discipline.

He can see our actions
as easily as we see the sun.

That's the reason
we can't hide our sins—
he can see them all.

But he knows what we are like,
and so he shows mercy
and never deserts us.[33]

The Lord values
our gifts to the poor
as much as we value fine jewelry
or a most prized possession.

Later, he will punish the wicked
as they deserve,

but he accepts all those
who turn from sinning,
and he encourages everyone
whose hope is slipping away.

ⓩ Stop sinning and return
to the Lord;
your punishment will be less
if you pray to him.

So turn back to the Lord
and hate what he hates.

ⓐ Only the living
can thank the Lord—
no one in the world of the dead
can praise him;

they no longer exist
and cannot offer thanks.
But those who are alive
and healthy
can still sing praises to him.

The Lord will show great mercy
and forgive all of your sins
if you turn back to him.

But we cannot live forever;
we are human and find it hard
to turn to God.

If a thick dark cloud can hide
the bright rays of the sun,
then evil can just as easily
darken our minds.

We are merely dust and ashes,
but God commands the armies
of the highest heaven.

The Lord, who lives forever,
created all things,

and he alone
is always faithful.
He is the only true God;

one signal from his hand
and the universe obeys.
The Lord, its mighty King,
decides what we
are allowed to use,
and what he will keep
for himself alone.[34]

We cannot understand
or tell other people
what God's power has done,

because we cannot measure it
or count the times
he has shown us mercy.

Nor can we make that number
larger or smaller.
We just stand in amazement
at the things he has done,

and at the end of a lifetime
of studying them,
we are still confused.

As for us humans, what are we?
Do we have any purpose?
What is good for us
and what is bad?
Few of us live a hundred years,

which is a very short time
when compared to forever,
like a drop of ocean water
or a grain of sand
along a beach.

That's why the Lord
patiently shows us mercy,
time after time.

He is eager to forgive,
because he knows that we
are condemned to die.

We show mercy to our friends,
but the Lord has mercy
on everyone.
He corrects and disciplines
and teaches us,
and leads us back to him
like a shepherd
leading a flock of sheep.

If we pay attention
when he corrects us,
and if we study his laws,
he will be merciful.

Students, be careful
not to ruin a gift
by criticizing the person
you give it to.

Like a refreshing dew
in the heat of summer,
saying the right thing
can mean more than giving

even a generous gift.
But a truly gracious person
will be ready with both.

So don't be a fool.
People's eyes will sparkle,
if your generosity is sincere
and you speak with kindness.

Know what you're talking about
before you start speaking
and look after your health
before it fails.

If you turn from sin now—
before you are judged—
you will find forgiveness.

Be humble and show that you
have turned from sin,
and you won't be punished
with illness.

If you promise the Lord a gift,
give it now.
Don't wait until you are dying
to keep your promise,

and don't make promises to him
unless you are ready
to keep them.
Don't test the Lord's patience.

When your time comes to die
and face him as judge,
if he is angry, he will refuse
to even look at you.

If you have grown wealthy
and have plenty of food,
remember when you yourself
were poor and hungry.

Between sunrise and sunset
is plenty of time for the Lord
to turn everything around.

The wise are always careful,
and they make sure to do right
when others are sinning.

Smart people recognize Wisdom,
and they praise everyone
who finds her.

If you study the writings
of the wise,
you will learn enough Wisdom
to share with others.

[35]Control your desires;
don't let them control you.

If you allow yourself to enjoy
everything you want,
you will look like a fool,
and your enemies will laugh.

Do not live in luxury
that you cannot afford,
or you will become poor.

If you have nothing,
don't borrow money
to give a banquet.

A worker who is always drunk
will never get rich,
and people who are careless
with small things
will gradually slip
into poverty.

Even a wise man can be deceived
by wine and women,
and men who visit prostitutes
are taking terrible risks.

Death will come suddenly,
then their bodies will rot
and be eaten by worms.

If you trust people too quickly,
you aren't very smart.
If you sin, you harm yourself.

And if you are happy about evil,
you will be judged guilty.

Trouble doesn't stay
where gossip is hated.

It doesn't cost you a thing
to forget a rumor.

So don't tell it to your enemies
or even your friends,
unless you would be doing wrong
by not speaking up.

People may be listening
and watching,
and if you repeat gossip,
they might start hating you.

Treat rumors as secrets
to be kept until you die;
they can even make you feel
as though you might explode,
but be brave—you won't.

It's harder for a fool
not to repeat gossip,
than for a woman in labor
not to give birth

or a soldier to ignore an arrow
sticking out of his leg. *

Talk to friends or neighbors
if you hear that they
have wronged you
or said something to harm
your reputation.

If they didn't do it,
you will find out,
and if they did,
they won't do it again.

Talk to them and get the truth,
because you should not believe
everything you hear.

We are all guilty
of saying things
that we didn't really mean.

So don't threaten your friends
before you talk with them,
and always obey the Law
of God Most High.

ⓑ If you fear the Lord,
he will accept you,
and if you are wise,
he will give you his love.

If you learn and follow
the Lord's commands,
you will please him,
and he will allow you to eat
fruit from the tree
that lets you live forever.[36]

Fearing the Lord
and obeying his Law
are really the same
as having wisdom.

If slaves refuse to do
what they are told,
their master will be angry
even if they later obey.

Sin isn't the source of Wisdom,
and so sinners cannot give
good advice.

Some people are clever
in doing disgusting things;
others are fools only because
they never learned wisdom.

It is better to fear the Lord
and not be so intelligent,
than to be very smart
and to break his Law.

Some people are highly skilled
at being unfair
and can even twist your kindness
to help them win
their case in court.

Some dishonest people
act like they are mourning,

hiding their faces from you
and pretending not to listen.
But they will use your words
to take advantage of you
when no one is looking.

They may not be able
to do so now,
but they will wrong you
the first chance they get.

Merely by looking at people
you know what they are like.
And the first time you meet,
you can tell if someone
has good sense

by what they wear
and how they walk or laugh.

If you are smart,
you will know
when to keep quiet
and when to criticize.

It is better to speak up
than to let anger
eat away at you.

When you are wrong, admit it,
and you will save yourself
a lot of trouble.

If a man is unable to have sex,
he can't be forced to do so;
but it seems harder to force
some people to do right.

People who talk too much
are not well liked,
but people who keep quiet
are often considered wise.

Their silence may just mean
they have nothing to say.
Or it may mean they are waiting
for the right time to speak,

and that is true wisdom.
But fools who always talk
about themselves
are never quiet.

They are disgusting,
and others who grab for power
also earn our hatred.

Sometimes our troubles
end up helping us,
and other times, good luck
turns out to be bad.

Often a gift
isn't really helpful,
and when some people give,
they expect to be repaid
twice what the gift is worth.

If you search for glory
you may lose what you have,
but success can come to those
who had humble beginnings.

What seems to be a bargain
may cost you seven times
what it is worth.

People love to hear
wise people speak.
But fools, though polite,
are still fools,

and you would be better off
without gifts from them,
because they will expect you
to give even more in return.

A small gift, they think,
gives them the right
to criticize—loudly.
Today, they make a loan;
tomorrow, they ask
for their money back.
I cannot stand people like this!

Fools think they have no friends
and that no one appreciates
their good deeds.
They invite you over for a meal
but believe that you will later
say bad things about them.

They deserve to be mocked,
and few people
will pass up the chance.

It is better to slip and fall
than to suffer a slip
of the tongue.
And something that small
can bring sudden disaster
to those who are evil.

A rude person is disgusting,
just like the dirty stories
ignorant people tell.

Everyone ignores a proverb
repeated by a fool,
because fools never choose
the right one.

You may be too poor
to commit some sins,
but you can sleep at night
with a clear conscience.

Have a little self-respect
and don't act like a fool,
or you may risk disaster.

Broken promises turn friends
into enemies,
so do not make promises
that you cannot keep.

The ignorant are always lying,
not knowing that telling a lie
leaves an ugly scar.

Liars are worse than thieves,
but both are on the road
to destruction.

Telling lies brings disgrace
and shame that never ends.

[37]If you are wise
and want to succeed,
you will try to please
those in power.

If you plow and plant,
you will harvest a crop,
and if you please the powerful,
they will overlook
your mistakes.

Favors and gifts keep the wise
from seeing the truth
and muzzle the mouths of those
who criticize.
*

Wisdom kept secret
and wealth never used
do not help anybody,

but a fool does best
to keep foolishness hidden.

Students, if you
are sinning, stop.
Ask God to forgive you.

Sin is a deadly snake
with lion's fangs.
Stay away! If you come close
you will be bitten.

Lawlessness kills
like a sword with two edges
swung this way and that.

If you are too shy
or proud and arrogant,
you can lose your wealth
and even your home.

ⓒ The prayers of the poor
go straight to God's ear,
and he quickly answers.

If you hate correction,
you will become a sinner,
but if you take it to heart,
you will obey the Lord.

Good speakers can be famous,
but people with common sense
will catch every mistake.

Borrowing to build your house
is like gathering stones
to mark your own grave.

A meeting of evil people
is a bundle of straw,
where a small flame
ends in a blazing fire.

For a sinner, the road of life
is smooth, but it slopes down
to the world of the dead.

Fear the Lord in every way,
and you will become wise;
Control your thoughts,
and you can obey his Law.

You have to be smart to learn,
but some kinds of smartness
only make other people bitter.

The knowledge of the wise
is an overflowing river,
and their advice
is a life-giving spring.

The mind of a fool
is a leaking jar
that holds nothing for long.

When the wise hear a proverb,
they give it praise
and add more to it;
but fools merely laugh
and forget it.

Listening to a fool is tiring,
like carrying a heavy backpack
on a journey,
but listening to someone smart
is refreshing.

In a town meeting, people look
for some common sense,
and when they hear it,
they will think about
what was said.

Knowledge means nothing
to ignorant fools,
and wisdom is useless
as a burned-down house.

Fools think learning puts chains
on their hands and feet,

and they laugh at it loudly.
But smart people just smile,

because they know that learning
is a golden bracelet.

Fools will push past you
into your home,
but the wise will wait
at your door.
*

They are polite and will not try
to see inside
or to hear what is going on,

because that would be rude
and shameful.

Some people won't stop talking
about things that are none
of their business.
But sensible people
weigh each word
before it is spoken.

In a fool, the mind thinks
only after the mouth speaks,
but in a wise person,
the mind controls the mouth.

The ungodly fall under any curse
that they try to place
on their enemies.

If you spread gossip,
your character will be ruined
and your neighbors
will hate you.

Lazy people
are like stones
that everyone spits on,

or a pile of manure
that you have to walk around.
If you touch them,
you want to wash your hands.

A father is ashamed if his son
isn't well behaved,
and a disobedient daughter
is even worse.

If a daughter is sensible
she will get a good husband,
but a shameless girl
only brings her father pain.

If a woman insults her father
and her husband,
they will be disgraced
and in return
will humiliate her.

Picking the wrong time
to criticize your children
is like playing happy music
at a funeral,
but punishment and correction
are always wise.

Bring up your children well
and no one will guess
you were poor as a child.

But rude and arrogant children
are a disgrace
to a good family.[38]

Just try to wake someone
from deep sleep,
to fix shattered pottery,
or to teach fools!

Tell them a story,
and they will doze off;
and afterwards, they will ask,
“So what's your point?”

Mourn for the dead,
who rest in darkness,
but weep even more for fools,
doomed to live
in the darkness of stupidity.

ⓓ After a death,
you mourn seven days,
but you mourn for ungodly fools
until the day they die.

To avoid lots of problems
and have a peaceful life,
stay away from stupid people
and do not talk to them.
When a wet dog shakes itself,
water flies everywhere,
but humans who have no sense
splatter everyone nearby
with troubles.

A fool is a heavier burden
than a load of lead,

or a bag of sand or salt,
or a lump of iron ore.

An earthquake cannot loosen
a wooden beam set firmly
in the walls of a building,
and no crisis can shake you up
if you have prepared your mind
and decided what you must do.

A mind that thinks
and reasons well
is like a smooth wall
decorated with carvings.[39]

But foolish cowards
will not stand firm
when fearsome disaster hits,
just as small rocks are blown
from the top of a vineyard wall[40]
by the wind.

Poke someone in the eye
and they will shed tears;
but wound their heart
and they will show deep pain.

You can throw stones
to frighten birds away,
and you can end a friendship
by tossing insults.

Threaten a friend with a sword,
and you can still hope
the friendship will heal.

And you can argue with friends
and later, win them back.
But if you are arrogant
and insult them,
if you tell their secrets
or harm them
when they trusted you,
they will be gone for good.

Earn the trust of your neighbors
when they are poor,
and if they become wealthy
you can celebrate with them.
Be a true friend
when they have troubles,
and later they will share
their inheritance with you.

When you smell smoke
you will soon see flames,
and when you hear insults,
you will soon see murder.

I will never be ashamed
to protect my friends,
and I will always be there
when I am needed.

But if they turn and harm me,
all who find out
will be on their guard
against them.

Our Lord, help me to be careful
in everything I say,
so that my mouth
will not be my downfall.

You are my Father
and rule my life—
don't let my mouth
control my destiny
and bring me down.

Please send wisdom
to whip my thoughts
and train my mind,
to tell me when I have sinned,
and to correct me
when I am wrong.

Otherwise, I will sin more often
and make more mistakes,
allowing my enemies
to destroy me and celebrate.

Our Lord, you are God;
you gave me life.
Don't let me look with arrogance
at other people.

Help me not to want things
that are not rightfully mine.

And don't let me be controlled
by shameless desires
for food or sex
or anything else.

[41]Students, if you listen to me
and control what you say,
your words cannot trap you.

Sinners are caught
when they talk too much,
and the arrogant are tripped up
because they insult others.

ⓔ Don't get in the habit
of using God's name
when you make promises.

Every time you do this,
God starts watching you closely
as a master watches a slave,
and he will notice all your sins
and punish you for them.

The wicked make promises
with God as their witness,
but when a promise is broken,
they just explain
that it was made by mistake.
Or they forget it on purpose,
which means that they
are twice as guilty.
And if they lied in God's name,
they will not be forgiven
and their family is doomed. *

Vulgar talk is sinful
and deadly.
Godly people do not enjoy sin,
so they don't use evil words,
and I pray that none
will ever be heard in Israel.

Avoid sin
by training your mouth
not to use bad language,

and always remember
your parents will be honored
if you behave properly.
Then when you meet
with important people,
you won't get excited
and start talking like a fool
and end up wishing
you had never been born.

As long as you are in the habit
of insulting others,
you will always be a fool.

Three kinds of sinners
bring down God's anger:
first are those who always burn
with desire for sex.
Their desire is a flame
that must burn itself out.
Next are those
who won't stop having sex
with a near relative.
God will send fire
to destroy them!

Last are those who have sex
with anyone they can
and will do so
until they die.

A man unfaithful to his wife
says to himself,
“It's dark outside,
and no one can see me here
inside the house.
Why worry, anyway?
God will forgive me.”

People like that are only afraid
of being seen by others
and forget that God's eyes shine
ten thousand times brighter
than the sun.
He can see every hidden place
in our lives.

He knew all things
before he created them,
just as he knows them now.

So unfaithful husbands
will be caught
when they suspect nothing
and will be punished
in the city streets.

If an unfaithful wife
has a child by another man,
she will be punished.

She has broken God's Law
and sinned against her husband
by having sex with another man
and giving birth to his child.

She will be put on trial
in a town meeting,
and she will be punished.
Even her children will suffer,

because they will be rejected
by the people of the town.

Her shame will never be erased,
and people will curse
when they remember her.

But they will be reminded
that it is always best
to fear the Lord
and obey his commands.

* Wisdom sings her own praise
and tells her greatness

to those who worship the Lord
and to the Lord's armies
in heaven.

And this is what she says:
I came from the mouth
of God Most High
and like a mist I moved
over the earth.[42]

ⓕ Highest heaven was my home,
and my throne was a cloud.

Only I could travel
around the horizon
and walk in ocean depths.

I ruled every nation,
the land, and the sea.

But where could I find
a place to settle down?
In what nation should I live?

Then the Creator of the world
gave me a command
telling me to set up my tent
among the descendants of Jacob,
the people of Israel.

He created me ages ago,
before time began,
and I will never die.

I served in his sacred tent,
and later in the temple
in Zion.[43]

He gave me a resting place
in Jerusalem, the city he loves,
and this is where I rule.

Here, I put down roots
among the glorious people
who belong to the Lord.

I grew tall, like a cedar tree
in Lebanon
or a cypress on Mount Hermon;

like a palm tree at En-Gedi,
or the roses in Jericho;
like a beautiful olive tree
in a field,
or like an evergreen.

My perfume smelled sweet
like the spices cinnamon and cane
mixed with fine myrrh,[44]
or like smoke in the sacred tent
from the incense of galbanum,
onycha, and stacte.[45]

I spread out my branches,
beautiful and graceful,
like a giant oak,

and like a grapevine,
they grew buds and blossoms,
and finally clusters of rich,
sweet fruit.

Beautiful love is my child,
and so are fear and knowledge
and hope that trusts God,
and he gives me and my children
to his own people.[46]

So come, if you want,
and enjoy my fruit
until you are full.

I am sweeter than honey
straight from the honeycomb,
and the sweet taste will remain
even when you have finished.

But the more of me
that you eat and drink,
the more you will want.

Obey me and let me work
in your life,
and you will never sin
or be disgraced.

The wisdom of God Most High
is the Book of the Law
of Moses,
the agreement God made
with our ancestors
and gives to each new generation
as an inheritance.

Always stay close
to the Lord All-Powerful, and he
will give you strength.
The Lord is the only true God—
he alone can rescue.[47] *

God's wisdom and understanding
overflow from his Law
just as in early springtime,[48]
the Tigris and Pishon Rivers,

the Euphrates and the Jordan,
overflow their banks.

God's teachings flood the land[49]
like the Nile River[50]
in early fall.[51]

The first human did not know
Wisdom very well,
nor will the last one,

because her thoughts
are wider than the ocean,
and her advice is deeper
than the sea.

I thought of myself
as a small canal
from the river of God's Wisdom
to my garden,

bringing water for my flowers
and fruit trees.
But soon this canal
became wide as a river
and then as the sea.

And so, I will teach wisdom
and make it shine like sunrise,
clearly seen from far away.

My words flow quickly,
and like a prophet
I will leave them
for generations not yet born.

You see, my work hasn't been
for myself alone,
but for everyone who desires
to find Wisdom.

Three things
make me happy
and are beautiful to the Lord
and to humans:
a family that agrees,
neighbors who are friends,
and a happy marriage.

But I hate three kinds of people
and the way they live:
the poor that brag,
the rich that tell lies,
and the old that are foolish
and unfaithful in marriage.

If you don't start learning
when you are young,
you will have no wisdom
when you grow old.

Good sense and sound advice
are beautiful in those
whose hair is now gray.

Wisdom and understanding
are just right for the old
and honorable.

They have seen so many things,
but they only boast
that they fear the Lord.

Ten kinds of people
can be called truly happy:
those who enjoy their children,
and those who live long enough
to see their enemies fall;

men who have sensible wives,
farmers that do not hitch
a donkey and an ox
to one plow,[52]
people who don't sin
by what they say,
and servants whose masters
have as much ability
as the servants do;

people with common sense,
and those who speak to others
who are really listening.
*

The person who finds wisdom
finds great happiness,
but happiest of all is the one
who fears the Lord,

because fearing him
is more important
than anything else.

And it is the first step
in loving him,
just as having faith
is the first step in becoming
one of his followers.[53]

The breaking of your heart
by an evil woman
is the most painful
of all injuries.

No suffering seems worse
than when an enemy hates you
and takes revenge,

but a woman's anger is strongest,
just as snakes have
the strongest poison.

I would be happier to live
with a lion and a dragon
than to be married
to an evil woman.

An evil woman's face is changed
into something dark,
like the face of a bear.

Her husband is depressed,
sighing bitterly
even among his friends.

The sins of men are small
compared to those of women,
and I pray that women who sin
will be severely punished.

It is easier for an old person
to climb a hill of sand
than for a quiet man to live
with a chattering wife.

Do not be taken captive
by the beauty of a woman,
and don't desire her wealth.[54]

When the wife's possessions
support the family,
their home will be filled
with anger, insults,
and shame.

An evil wife brings no happiness
to her husband;
instead, she makes him sad
and depressed;
she breaks his heart,
and he is afraid
to make any decisions.

Sin began with the first woman,
and because of her
we all must die.

Do not let water leak
from your storage jars,
and if your wife is evil,
don't let her talk in public.

If she will not obey you,
then get a divorce.

A man who is married
to a good woman
is happy, and he will live
twice as long.

If a wife has courage,
her husband will find happiness
and live a peaceful life.

A good wife is the best gift
given by the Lord
to those men who fear him,

and whether such a man
is rich or poor,
the smile on his face
comes from a happy heart.

I am always extra careful
when I hear ugly gossip
being repeated in the city,
or when a mob comes together,
or when the innocent
are falsely accused.
I would rather die than be part
of those three things.
But a fourth is even worse:

to have two wives,
with one of them being jealous
of the other.
She can only bring heartache
and sorrow,
as her words cut the family
to pieces.

To marry an evil woman
is to hold a scorpion
in your hand,
or to wear an ox-yoke[55]
that rubs your shoulders raw.

When a wife gets drunk
she can be unfaithful,
and then her husband's anger
will explode.

You know a wife is unfaithful
if her eyes have a look
of invitation. *

If your daughter is rebellious
and wants her own way,
watch her closely

and don't be surprised
if she sins against you
the first chance she gets.

She will try to have sex
with every man she sees,
just as thirsty travelers
will drink any water
they can find.

A gracious wife is pleasing
to her husband,
and her cooking skills
keep him fat.[56]

Her silence is a gift
from the Lord,
and her self-control
is without price.

Her modesty is the crown
on all her graces;
her faithfulness to her husband
has value beyond measure.

She has organized her home,
and her beauty there
is like the sun as it rises
in the Lord's heaven.

A lovely face on a good figure
shines like the lamps
on the sacred lampstand,

and shapely legs with firm feet
are like golden columns
resting on silver bases.

Students, when you come of age,
stay away from immoral women
and stay healthy.[57]

To find a good wife
is to find a good field
where you can plant
your own seed,
knowing that it will produce
a good harvest.

And as your children grow,
they will have confidence
in your family line
and will become successful.

A prostitute is worth
less than spit,
and affairs with married women
are deadly.

Reject God's Law,
and he will give you
a godless wife.
But if you fear the Lord,
your wife will be
faithful to him.

A shameless wife is vulgar
in public,
but a good woman is modest,
even alone with her husband.

If a wife does what she pleases,
she is no better than a dog,
but if she is ashamed
to do wrong,
she will fear the Lord.

Everyone can tell
that a wife is wise
if she honors her husband,
but if she is proud and refuses,
they will see she is ungodly.
Yes, a man who is married
to a good woman
is happy, and he will live
twice as long.

But the constant talking
of a loud wife
is a trumpet signal
for a battle to begin,
and her family will live
in the chaos of war.[58]

It makes me terribly sad
when the rich become poor
or the wise are insulted.
But I get angry
if those who do right
start doing wrong.
The Lord will punish them
with a violent death.

It is almost impossible
to succeed in business
and still be honest.

People sin
when they try to get rich,
and they convince themselves
they are not being dishonest.

But sin is stuck tightly
between buying and selling,
just like a wooden peg
hammered between two stones
in a wall.

And if you disobey the Lord,
your home[59]
will soon be destroyed.

Impurities can easily be seen
after flour is sifted
and after someone speaks.

The quality of a clay dish
is seen in the heat
of the pottery furnace,
just as our true character
is seen in what we say.

ⓖ Fruit shows how much care
a tree has received,
and our words show the care
we have given to our minds.

So don't start praising people,
until you hear what they say.

Make fairness your goal;
you will reach it
and receive great honor.

Just as birds of the same kind
come together in a flock,
people will be honest with you,
if you are honest with them.

But sin is watching like a lion
and is ready to gobble you up
if you do evil.

Good people always speak wisely,
but you never can tell
what a fool will say next.

Don't waste your time
listening to stupid people;
spend it with those
who know how to think.

It is disgusting to listen to fools
laughing about their sins.
*

Their cursing and arguing
makes your hair stand on end
and your ears ache.

But fools are too proud
to stop sinning,
until someone gets hurt.

ⓗ Your friends won't trust you
or like you anymore
if you can't keep a secret.

If you don't tell their secrets,
your friends will know
that you really like them.
But if you tell their secrets,
they won't want you around,

and your friendship is as dead
as a soldier
killed by the enemy.

It is gone for good,
like a captive bird
that has been set free

or a deer that has escaped
from a trap.
So don't bother trying
to get back that friendship.

Wounds can be bandaged
and insults forgiven,
but nothing can heal the harm
of telling someone's secret.

Stay away from those who wink
and plan to deceive others.

They tell you how great you are
and how well you speak,
but later, they twist your words
to make you look bad.

I hate people like them
more than anything else,
and even the Lord hates them.

Striking a friend is the same
as throwing a stone
straight up in the air—
you will be the one
who gets hurt.

And you will get caught
in traps that you set
to cheat others.

No one seems to realize
that evil always returns
to the one who does it.

Proud people insult others,
but revenge will follow them
like a lion stalking a victim.

When God's people
are in trouble,
those who make fun of them
will die in terrible pain,
like an animal in a trap.

Taking revenge in anger
is disgusting—yet sinners
just won't give it up.

The Lord keeps a record
of those who take revenge
and he will punish them
for their sins.

ⓘ So forgive those who harm you,
and when you pray,
God will forgive you.

But don't expect
the Lord to forgive you
if you hold a grudge
or refuse to show mercy.

God won't accept sacrifices
for your sins
if you stay angry with others.

Life is too short
to waste it by being angry,
so obey God
and forgive your enemies.

Remember his commands
and his agreement with us.
Don't let the faults of others
make you angry with them.

If you control your temper,
you won't start arguments
or sin as much.

And remember, it is sinful
to start an argument
that destroys a friendship.

Stubbornness heats up
an argument,
just as adding wood
heats up a fire.
If you are rich and powerful,
you can afford
to lose your temper.

A quick temper can blaze up
and lead to murder.

Blow on a spark
and it bursts into flames;
spit on a spark
and it dies out.
In the same way, your words
can either heat up an argument
or make it cool down.

ⓙ Those who gossip and tell lies
are under God's curse,
because they have brought ruin
to innocent people

and forced many others
to leave home and country.
Such talk has been the downfall
of strong cities
and powerful leaders.

Because of lies others told,
faithful, hard-working wives
have been divorced
and have lost everything[60]

So if you want peace of mind,
don't listen to gossip!

Whips leave painful stripes
on the skin,
but cruel words break bones
deep down inside

and can be more deadly
than a sword.
*

You should be happy
if you have never been attacked
with lies and gossip,
or felt them wrap around you

like heavy bronze chains
or a collar[61] made of iron.

Cruel words cause suffering[62]
worse than death itself.

Although they are fiery flames,
they cannot harm
God's faithful people,

but those who reject him
will be burned to ashes.
And these cruel words
are like lions and leopards,
hunting down their victims
and tearing them apart.

You protect your land
with a hedge of thornbushes
and you keep your money
locked away safely.

Be just as careful
to guard what you say.
Always think before you speak,
and know when to be silent,

so that others cannot attack you
for what you have said.

ⓚ Obey God's Law
about helping
your neighbors;
have mercy and lend them money
when they truly need it.

If others lend you money,
pay them back,
and don't be late!

If you keep your promises
to pay what you owe,
others will be glad
to lend you what you need.

Many think what they borrow
is theirs to keep,
and this causes trouble
when others try to help them.

Those who lend money
are treated with great respect
until the time comes
for loans to be repaid.
Then all they get are excuses
and stories of hard times.

Sometimes they feel lucky
to get back half
of the money they lent.
And instead of respect,
a lender often gets nothing
but curses and angry words
from a borrower
who is robbing the lender
by not paying back the loan.

That's why many refuse to lend;
they do care about others,
but they have no desire
to be cheated.

However, give to the poor;
be quick and generous.

Obey the Lord's command—
don't refuse to help
when people are in need.

Give away your silver
to a brother or a friend;
don't hide it under a rock,
where it will rust.

Give your gold to the needy
as God Most High commands,
and you will be better off
than if you had kept it.

Your gifts to the poor
are a treasure
that will rescue you
when trouble comes,

and they give better protection
than the strongest shields
and spears.

When a friend needs a loan,
a good person will guarantee
that the loan will be repaid.
To refuse to help this friend
would be a disgrace.

And remember—if friends
have helped you this way,
they have risked all they own
to be kind *

and rescue you.
So if they are forced to repay
what you borrowed,

you are not only ungrateful,
you are a sinner.

People of wealth and power
have often become poor
and homeless,
drifting from place to place
in foreign countries,
their wealth taken in payment
for loans they guaranteed.

Sinners want to make big profits
from guaranteed loans,
but they may get only trouble
and lawsuits in court.

Be careful! There is danger
when you guarantee a loan,
but you have to help a friend
as much as you can.

Life's most important things
are water, food, clothing,
and a home that gives you
some privacy.

It is better to be poor
in your own home,
than to live with others
and be treated to banquets.

Be content with what you have,
because you will be insulted
if you live off others.[63]

You will always be miserable
and will not dare say a word,
if you have to depend on others
for food and a place to sleep.

You will become their servant,
and instead of thanking you,
they will say,

“Stranger, set the table!”
Or, “I'm still hungry—
give me your food.”

Finally they will tell you to leave
so they can give your room
to a relative
or some important guest.

Such comments are insulting
to a sensitive person,
just like those of a lender
demanding payment of a loan.

Parents show love
for their children
by spanking them
whenever they do wrong.
Later those parents will be glad
to see their children
grow into fine adults.

If parents correct a child,
they will someday boast
about how much help
that child has been to them.

Parents who give their children
an education
make their enemies jealous
and can boast to friends.
*

These parents have no regrets
when they die,
and they live on
in their children,
who brought them such joy.

Then it is up to the children
to take revenge
on their parents' enemies,
and to be kind
to their parents' friends.

If you spoil your children,
you will have to put bandages
on the wounds they will get,
and you will shake with fear
every time you hear shouting.

That's why you
must correct them,
so they will not be as stubborn
as unbroken horses.
*

If you give children
everything they desire
and laugh when they sin,

they will end up giving you
sadness, terror, and pain.

Don't let them do
just anything they want,
but correct their mistakes.

Break their stubbornness
by whipping them
while they are young,
or else they will disobey you
and bring you deep sorrow.

Take care to discipline them,
so they will not disgrace you
when they are older.

I would rather be poor,
yet healthy and strong,
than to be rich and sickly.

To have your health
and a good attitude[64]
is better than gold
and infinite riches.

No amount of money
can compare with health
or bring happiness
deep inside your heart.

Death is better
than a life of constant pain
and sickness.

If a fine dinner is served,
but the guests
are too sick to eat,
the dinner will go to waste,
like food placed on a grave[65]

or like a sacrifice to an idol,
which cannot eat or smell.
If the Lord punishes people
by making them sick,

they can only stare at food
and groan like a young man
unable to have sex
who holds a virgin
in his arms.

ⓛ Don't stay sad for too long
or look for reasons
to be upset.

Joy and happiness will help you
to live longer.

So enjoy life and forget sorrow,
because sorrow never helps,
and it has destroyed many.

Jealousy and anger
will cut your life short,
and worrying makes you old
before your time.

If you are cheerful at meals,
the food you eat
will do you more good.

The rich lie awake at night
worrying about their money,
and they waste away
from lack of rest.

It is hard to fall asleep
when you are very sick
or worried about money.

The rich work hard
and get richer,
then they rest in luxury.

But the poor work hard
and barely earn a living,
and if they rest,
they become even poorer.

Some people love gold
and aim to make big profits,
and they will be led to do wrong
instead of right.

Because of gold,
many lives have been ruined
or have ended in destruction.

Money controls those fools
who worship it.

God will bless those rich people
who do only what is right
and don't try to get richer.

If you ever find such people,
you should honor them
for doing the impossible.

When tested by money,
who can always do right?
When people could cheat
to get more money
but instead are honest,
they have something
to boast about.

The community will honor them
for the kind things they do,
and their wealth will grow.

When you go to a banquet,
don't stuff yourself
just because so much food
is on the table.

Some people's eyes
want everything they see.
Is anything more evil?
Such eyes will have plenty
of tears.

Don't reach out and grab
the food you want
or push others aside.

Be thoughtful of others—
they have feelings too.

Eat what is served,
but you won't be welcome
if you gobble it up
like an animal.

Show good manners;
stop eating before others do.
If you keep on eating,
they will be offended.

And in a large group of people,
don't be the first one
to reach for the serving dish.

Train yourself to be satisfied
with a little food,
and later, in bed,
you will not be in pain
when you try to breathe.

You will sleep well
and wake up early,
feeling good.
But overeating causes cramps,
as well as an upset stomach
and sleepless nights.

If your host makes you overeat,
leave the table and vomit—
you will feel much better.

Listen to me! Pay attention,
and later on,
you will thank me.
Don't try to eat[66] too much,[67]
and you will stay healthy.

It is right to honor those
who are generous with food
at their dinner parties.

But the whole town is right
to complain about a host
who gives only a snack.

Wine has destroyed many lives,
and heavy drinking is no way
to prove you are strong.

The strength of iron is seen
when it is heated,
then dipped in water.
And people show their arrogance
when they argue
after drinking wine.

A little wine is refreshing,
and to live without wine
is hardly living at all.
God created wine
to make us happy.

A little wine at the right time
helps us celebrate.

But drinking too much wine
leads us to hate others
and to take revenge.[68]

When fools are drunk,
they become angry and fight,
but their strength is gone,
and so they get hurt.

Don't scold your friends
or be disgusted
when they drink and celebrate
at a party.
And don't make them feel bad
or upset them with a reminder
that they owe you money.

* If you are placed in charge
